fallacies.name: ad_hominem
fallacies.description: Attacking the opponent rather than their argument.

fallacies.name: straw_man
fallacies.description: Misrepresenting the opponent's position to make it easier to attack.

fallacies.name: appeal_to_emotion
fallacies.description: Substituting emotional pressure for evidence or reasoning.

fallacies.name: false_dilemma
fallacies.description: Presenting only two options when more exist.

fallacies.name: hasty_generalization
fallacies.description: Drawing a broad conclusion from an unrepresentative sample.

fallacies.name: appeal_to_authority
fallacies.description: Treating an authority's opinion as proof without supporting evidence.

fallacies.name: slippery_slope
fallacies.description: Claiming one step inevitably leads to an extreme outcome without justification.

fallacies.name: circular_reasoning
fallacies.description: Using the conclusion as a premise of the argument.

fallacies.name: red_herring
fallacies.description: Introducing an irrelevant point to divert attention from the motion.

fallacies.name: unsupported_claim
fallacies.description: Asserting a factual claim without citing any source.
